article_title: Rolls-Royce SMR Signs Czech Republic Early Works Contract With ČEZ for Temelín, Cementing Status as Europe's Only Multi-Country SMR Vendor
summary: Substantively important Rolls-Royce SMR Czech deal — the lead facts (April 24 signing, Temelín site, ČEZ 20% stake, 3 GW partnership, second reactor after UK) all verify in the AP wire. But several supporting claims are attributed to sources that do not contain them: 'Wylfa' is never named in source-1 (gov.uk); '470-megawatt pressurised water reactor' is not in source-1; 'GE Hitachi and Holtec' rivals are not in source-2 (CNBC); 'South Bohemia' is not in source-0 (AP); the 'mid-decade projections' line is misattributed to AP; and the Beneš direct-quote fragments are actually AP paraphrases, not Beneš's verbatim words. The article needs source corrections or rewording.

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: abcnews.com
Wylfa is never named in source-1 (gov.uk)
findings.details: The article asserts the gov.uk announcement is for 'three reactors at Wylfa in North Wales' and references 'the UK's own Wylfa programme'. Source-1 (gov.uk) discusses the GBE-N × Rolls-Royce SMR contract for three units but does NOT name Wylfa. Either add a source that names Wylfa or rephrase to 'three reactors in the UK' without naming the site.

findings.message: '470-megawatt pressurised water reactor, 60-year operating lifetime' attributed to source-1 — only the lifetime is there
findings.details: Source-1 says 'more than 60 years' lifetime and '1.4GWe across three units' but does NOT say '470 MW per unit' nor 'pressurised water reactor'. Add a separate source for the PWR/470 MW specs or remove.

findings.message: 'beating out GE Hitachi and Holtec International' attributed to CNBC (source-2) — names not in snapshot
findings.details: Source-2 confirms June 2025 selection and 'two-year' selection process, but neither GE Hitachi nor Holtec is named. Either re-source or remove the named rivals.

findings.message: 'southern Bohemia' / 'South Bohemian region' attributed to AP — not in source-0
findings.details: Source-0 (AP) does not mention 'South Bohemia' or 'southern Bohemia'. The Temelín plant is in Týn nad Vltavou, which is in South Bohemia, but that geographical inference is not in the snapshot. Either drop the regional descriptor or cite a separate source.

findings.message: 'late 2030s, slightly later than earlier mid-decade projections cited in the AP report' — AP report contains no such projections
findings.details: Source-0 (AP) contains no 'mid-decade' or '2030s' projections. The only timeline AP cites is 'all the approvals by 2030'. The 'mid-decade projections' framing is fabricated. Drop the comparison or source it elsewhere.

findings.message: Beneš quotes presented as verbatim are actually AP paraphrases
findings.details: The article quotes Beneš directly: "the project plan and licensing documentation necessary for issuing building permits" and "to have all the approvals by 2030". In source-0 these phrases appear inside AP's paraphrasing structure ('Beneš said that the work includes...', 'Beneš said the company hopes to have...') — they are NOT presented as Beneš's direct words. Convert to indirect attribution: 'Beneš said the work covers project plans and licensing documentation needed for building permits, and that the company hopes to have all approvals by 2030'.

findings.message: 'grid connection targeted for the mid-2030s' attributed to gov.uk — actually in CNBC
findings.details: Source-1 (gov.uk) does not state a 'mid-2030s grid connection' target. Source-2 (CNBC) does say UK SMRs 'connected to the grid in the mid-2030s'. Move the citation.

content_preview.title: Rolls-Royce SMR Signs Czech Republic Early Works Contract With ČEZ for Temelín, Cementing Status as Europe's Only Multi-Country SMR Vendor
content_preview.summary: Rolls-Royce SMR and Czech utility ČEZ signed an early works contract on April 24 to begin design, licensing and site work for the country's first small modular reactor at Temelín, targeting up to 3 GW of capacity and grid connection in the late 2030s.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Czech state-controlled utility ČEZ Group and Rolls-Royce SMR signed an early works contract in Prague on April 24, 2026, formally beginning preparatory work to deploy the United Kingdom company's small modular reactor at the existing Temelín nuclear power plant in southern Bohemia, [the Associated Press reported](https://abcnews.go.com/Technology/wireStory/czech-power-company-ez-signs-deal-rolls-royce-132339038). The agreement covers design, licensing documentation, environmental as...

recommendations: Strip claims that aren't in the cited snapshots, or add additional sources for them.
Rewrite Beneš direct-quote fragments as indirect attribution to match how AP actually presented them.
Move the 'mid-2030s grid connection' citation from gov.uk to CNBC.

editor_notes.content_quality: Important Energy News piece (~851 words; well within News range). Clear structure (Overview / What We Know / Why an Early Works Contract Matters / What We Don't Know / Why It Matters), neutral tone, good context-setting on early-works contracts vs construction.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 3 snapshots. Source-0 (AP via ABC News) verifies: April 24 signing in Prague, ČEZ × Rolls-Royce SMR, existing Temelín site, ČEZ 20% stake, 'up to 3 gigawatt energy sources' partnership, 'second one built by the British firm' framing, 'all approvals by 2030'. Source-0 does NOT contain: 'South Bohemia' regional descriptor; 'mid-decade projections' for the timeline. The Beneš 'building permits' phrasing IS in source-0 but as AP paraphrase ('Beneš said that the work includes...'), not direct quote. Source-1 (gov.uk) verifies: April 13 contract signing, three units, '1.4GWe' across them, 'more than 60 years' lifetime. Source-1 does NOT contain: 'Wylfa' (never named); '470 MW per unit'; 'pressurised water reactor'; 'mid-2030s grid connection'. Source-2 (CNBC June 2025) verifies: June 10 2025 selection, 'two-year' competitive process, mid-2030s grid connection. Source-2 does NOT contain: GE Hitachi or Holtec rival names.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The lead facts (Prague, April 24, ČEZ × Rolls-Royce SMR, existing Temelín, 20% stake, 3 GW partnership, second reactor after UK) all verify cleanly. Several supporting details are misattributed or fabricated: Wylfa (not in source-1); 470 MW PWR specs (not in source-1); GE Hitachi/Holtec (not in source-2); South Bohemia (not in source-0); mid-decade projections framing (not in source-0); Beneš direct-quote fragments (paraphrases in AP, not verbatim quotes); mid-2030s grid connection citation should be CNBC not gov.uk.
editor_notes.originality: New event (April 24 signing) — distinct from the April 13 Wylfa article previously published. Internal cross-reference to that Wylfa article is appropriate.
concerns: Multiple claims attributed to sources that do not contain them.
Beneš paraphrases presented as verbatim direct quotes.
recommendations: Seven fixes per findings — most are minor wording or source-citation moves; the 'Wylfa' references and '470 MW PWR' specs need either a new source or to be dropped.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Important story, well-framed, but the source-attribution discipline is below The Machine Herald's standard. Fixes are mechanical. Ready for a clean rewrite.
